title: African Development Bank and JGC Corporation Sign Agreement to Advance Use of Sustainable Aviation Fuel and Africaâ€™s Green Aviation Agenda
content: The African Development Bank (AfDB) and Japanâ€™s JGC Corporation have signed a Letter of Intent to collaboratively advance sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) development and promote Africaâ€™s green aviation agenda. The agreement, formalized during the Tokyo International Conference on African Development, establishes a framework for partnership, joint promotion, knowledge exchange, technical studies, and co-financing. The AfDB will facilitate coordination with aviation stakeholders and project identification, while JGC will conduct feasibility studies and technical assessments, bringing Japanese clean energy technology to the continent. Both entities aim to accelerate Africaâ€™s energy transition, decarbonize aviation, and promote local economic growth and innovation. The partnership is aligned with AfDBâ€™s sustainable mobility and energy priorities and is seen as a key step towards making Africa a leader in green aviation. By converting waste and renewables into aviation fuel, the agreement could position Africa to supply the global SAF market, reduce emissions, and strengthen industrial competitiveness.
author: Analysts Account
